preamble: The voiceless alveolar lateral fricative /ɬ/, a relatively marked sound in the world's languages, can undergo a process of lenition, simplifying its articulation. One such transformation involves its shift to the voiceless glottal fricative /h/, a sound produced with minimal obstruction in the vocal tract.
phoneticEffects: This shift represents a form of debuccalization or glottalization, where the complex alveolar lateral articulation is lost, and the sound is reduced to a simple glottal fricative
sources: Schumann, O. (2019). Reconstructive Description of Eighteenth-century Xinka Grammar Volume I. LOT Publications.
